Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑}}. The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:白雲神社]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:白雲神社]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑}}. The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:白雲神社]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:白雲神社]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑}}. The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:白雲神社]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:白雲神社]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑}}. The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:白雲神社]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:白雲神社]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons Category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

{{noref|date=December 2019}} {{expand Japanese|topic=struct|白雲神社|date=December 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shirakumo Shrine | native_name = | native_name_lang = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirakkumojinja(Kyoto-shi).jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright = | alt = | caption = The shrine in 2014 | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | coordinates = | coordinates_footnotes = | religious_affiliation = Shinto | locale = | location = Kyoto | deity = | rite = | sect = | tradition = | festival = | cercle = | sector = | municipality = | district = | territory = | prefecture = | state = | province = | region = | country = Japan | administration = | consecration_year = | organisational_status = | functional_status = | heritage_designation = | ownership = | governing_body = | leadership = | bhattaraka = | patron = | religious_features_label = | religious_features = | architect = | architecture_type = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | creator = | funded_by = | general_contractor = | established = | groundbreaking = | year_completed = | construction_cost = | date_demolished = | facade_direction = | capacity = | length = | width = | width_nave = | interior_area = | height_max = | dome_quantity = | dome_height_outer = | dome_height_inner = | dome_dia_outer = | dome_dia_inner = | minaret_quantity = | minaret_height = | spire_quantity = | spire_height = | site_area = | temple_quantity = | monument_quantity = | shrine_quantity = | inscriptions = | materials = | elevation_m = | elevation_footnotes = | nrhp = | designated = | added = | refnum = | footnotes = | website = | name = Shirakumo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shirakkumojinja(Kyoto-shi).jpg|250px]]
Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|12=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|12=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|12=draft}}. The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

The shrine has no formal rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]

It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|12=draft|12=draft}} (unRanked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=draft|12=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|12=draft|12=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|12=draft|12=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|12=draft|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=draft|12=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=draft|12=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unRanked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]]

The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白雲神社 up to rev 103576110
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 白雲神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shirakkumojinja(Kyoto-shi).jpg|250px]]
本殿正面 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[京都御苑]]内 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 01 | 緯度秒 = 18.3 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 40.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Kyoto city2 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[イチキシマヒメ|妙音弁財天]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|無社格]] |創建 = 元仁元年（1224年） |本殿 = |別名 = 御所の弁天さん |例祭 = [[6月15日]] |神事 = 火焚祭（11月10日） }} '''白雲神社'''（しらくもじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]][[京都御苑]]内にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[無格社]]。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 妙音[[弁財天]]と称する[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]。[[西園寺家]]が[[琵琶]]の[[宗家]]であることから[[音楽]]の神として祀られてきた[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html 京都市 上京区の史蹟百選／白雲神社]。 == 歴史 == 当社は、そもそもは現在[[金閣寺]]がある場所に[[西園寺公経]]が建てた別荘・北山第の池の畔に[[元仁]]元年（[[1224年]]）に建てられた妙音堂がその始まりであるという。本尊として[[弁財天]]像が祀られていた他、[[西園寺家]]第二伝の[[空海|弘法大師]]筆と伝わる青龍妙音辨財天画像も祀られていたという[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/01_03_kyoto_gosho/demachi_benzaiten.php 京都に乾杯 出町妙音堂（妙音弁財天）とは？]。 弁財天像とともに祀られていた青龍妙音辨財天画像は、[[嘉元]]4年（[[1306年]]）4月に[[西園寺寧子]]（広義門院）が[[持明院統]]の[[後伏見天皇|後伏見上皇]]の後宮に女御として輿入れした際、自らの念持仏として御所に持っていき、現在は[[上京区]]の[[妙音堂]]の本尊となっている[https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/2853 KYOTO design 妙音堂]。 西園寺家の別荘・北山第の妙音堂は『[[増鏡]]』、『[[中務内侍日記]]』、『[[太平記]]』などに記述されており当時の風雅な佇まいが伺われる[https://kyotonikanpai.com/spot/01_03_kyoto_gosho/shirakumo_jinja.php 京都に乾杯 白雲神社（御所の弁天さん）]。 しかし、[[足利義満]]が[[西園寺実永]]から北山第を買収して自らの邸宅・[[北山殿]]を建築する際に妙音堂は破却された。その後、弁財天像は赤八幡京極寺にしばらく移されたという。 [[江戸時代]]の[[明和]]6年（[[1769年]]）に前[[内大臣]][[西園寺公晃]]と前[[右大臣]][[西園寺賞季]]が勅許を賜り、西園寺邸の移転とともに現在地に妙音堂を再興している。 [[天保]]11年（[[1840年]]）と[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の社殿修築に際しては、禁中各御所より寄付を賜って禁裏御祈祷所に定められている。 [[明治]]時代になると西園寺家が[[東京]]へ移転していまい残された妙音堂は[[廃仏毀釈]]によって廃寺の危機が訪れるが、地元有志の尽力により神社として存続することとなった。[[1878年]]（明治11年）に本尊の弁財天を[[宗像三女神]]の1柱である[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]に変更して祭神とし、かつての鎮座地の白雲村の旧称をとって白雲神社に名称を改めた。 [[1869年]]（明治2年）に当社の境内地に開いた私塾「立命館」の名は、現在の[[立命館大学]]へと継承されている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 薬師石 - 御所のへそ石と呼ばれる。 === 摂末社 === * 福寿[[稲荷神社]] == 分霊について == [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、京都衣笠村[[等持院]]字滝ヶ鼻（現・京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]]等持院東町近く。[[立命館大学]]衣笠キャンパス東門南側）にあった[[西園寺公望]]の別邸「萬介亭」を購入した[[臼居万太郎]]は、邸内の一隅に京都御苑内にある西園寺家本邸の「白雲神社」の分霊を勧請した。「萬介亭」には竹が植えてあったことから、この別邸を気に入っていた西園寺公望はこの別邸にあやかり自らを「竹軒」と号するようになった。白居万太郎氏はこの新邸を「時雨庵」と命名した。その後「萬介亭」は取り壊され、跡地には長年「西園寺公邸址」と掘られた石碑（地上部分高さ1メートル、幅・奥行23センチ）」が残されるのみとなった。この石碑も[[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）前後に撤去されたが、現在は[[学校法人立命館]]に保管されている[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 ＜懐かしの立命館＞竹軒西園寺公望と「萬介亭」2014年3月17日（立命館史資料センター準備室ウェブページ）]。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 木造[[弁才天]]坐像（西園寺妙音堂旧本尊）（彫刻） - [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）6月26日指定[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp　国指定文化財等データベース]2018年9月17日　閲覧。 == 所在地 == * 京都府京都市上京区京都御苑内 == アクセス == * [[京都市営地下鉄]][[京都市営地下鉄烏丸線|烏丸線]] [[丸太町駅]]より徒歩約9分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク　== *[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html 京都市 上京区の史蹟百選／白雲神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらくもしんしや きようとし}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]]
