revision.text:
{{noref|date=December 2019}} {{expand Japanese|topic=struct|白雲神社|date=December 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shirakumo Shrine | native_name = | native_name_lang = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirakkumojinja(Kyoto-shi).jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright = | alt = | caption = The shrine in 2014 | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | coordinates = | coordinates_footnotes = | religious_affiliation = Shinto | locale = | location = Kyoto | deity = | rite = | sect = | tradition = | festival = | cercle = | sector = | municipality = | district = | territory = | prefecture = | state = | province = | region = | country = Japan | administration = | consecration_year = | organisational_status = | functional_status = | heritage_designation = | ownership = | governing_body = | leadership = | bhattaraka = | patron = | religious_features_label = | religious_features = | architect = | architecture_type = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | creator = | funded_by = | general_contractor = | established = | groundbreaking = | year_completed = | construction_cost = | date_demolished = | facade_direction = | capacity = | length = | width = | width_nave = | interior_area = | height_max = | dome_quantity = | dome_height_outer = | dome_height_inner = | dome_dia_outer = | dome_dia_inner = | minaret_quantity = | minaret_height = | spire_quantity = | spire_height = | site_area = | temple_quantity = | monument_quantity = | shrine_quantity = | inscriptions = | materials = | elevation_m = | elevation_footnotes = | nrhp = | designated = | added = | refnum = | footnotes = | website = | name = Shirakumo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shirakkumojinja(Kyoto-shi).jpg|250px]]
Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}}

location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:UnRanked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Mukakusha]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|qq=}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Gyoen|qq=}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji family|qq=}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kintsune|qq=}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmochi|qq=}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutamachi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Subway|qq=}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|qq=}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Gyoen|qq=}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji family|qq=}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kintsune|qq=}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmochi|qq=}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutamachi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Subway|qq=}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = {{ill|June 15|ja|6月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|qq=}} | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen|ja|京都御苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Gyoen|qq=}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji family|qq=}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kintsune|qq=}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmochi|qq=}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutamachi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Subway|qq=}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|ja|西園寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji family|qq=}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|ja|西園寺公経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kintsune|qq=}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|ja|西園寺公望|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmochi|qq=}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|ja|立命館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsumeikan University|qq=}}.[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|ja|丸太町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutamachi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyoto City Subway|ja|京都市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Subway|qq=}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|ja|護王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]
