Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Hokora_or_Ungraded_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Hokora_or_Ungraded_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]]

The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto|List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]]

Front of the main hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 01 | Latitude seconds = 18.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 40.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | Label position = left | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Myōon Benzaiten) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Mukakusha|ja|無格社|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Hokora_or_Ungraded_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukakusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (unranked under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) | Established = 1224 (Gen'nin 1) | Main hall = | Alternate name = Gosho no Benten-san (common name) | Annual festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan. == deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=}} music.[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018. == History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den). It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine." In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]]. == Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018. == Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto|List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]]

location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds. ; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]]. ; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto|List of Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] ==External links== * {{Commons category-inline|Shirakumo-jinja}} {{coord missing|Kyoto Prefecture}} {{Japan-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白雲神社|version=103576110|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]][[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[ja:白雲神社]] [[en:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]] [[arz:ضريح شيراكومو]] {{Commons category|Shirakumo-jinja}} [[simple:Shirakumo Shrine]] [[zh:白云神社]]

15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

15|June 15]] | Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|qq=|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|qq=|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

[[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. '''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].

The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}
