'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].

Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

Rituals = Hitaki Festival (November 10) }} '''Shirakumo Shrine''' (白雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside the [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen]]. The shrine has no formal Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden]], in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] of the [[:en:Saionji family|Saionji family]], established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by [[:en:Saionji Kintsune|Saionji Kintsune]] as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), [[:en:Saionji Kinmochi|Saionji Kinmochi]] established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by [[:en:Ritsumeikan University|Ritsumeikan University]].<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from [[:en:Marutamachi Station|Marutamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Municipal Subway|Kyoto City Subway]].
; Nearby sites * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goō Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, inside the {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen}}. The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}.
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden}}, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway}}.

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden}}, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.

Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden}}, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=Pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family|1=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune|1=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi|1=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>

; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station|1=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway|1=Kyoto Municipal Subway}}.

== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=Pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family|1=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune|1=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi|1=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station|1=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway|1=Kyoto Municipal Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine|1=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|1=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden}}, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=Pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family|1=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune|1=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi|1=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station|1=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway|1=Kyoto Municipal Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine|1=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

The shrine has no formal Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
'''Shirakumo Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|en|Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|WD=Q843854|lt=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden|1=Kyoto Gyoen National Garden}}, in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, Japan.
== deity == The deity enshrined is {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, known as Myōon Benzaiten, revered as a deity of music due to the Saionji family’s status as the head family of {{ill|biwa|ja|琵琶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/biwa|1=Pipa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6685124}} music.<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku">[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kamigyo/page/0000012393.html Kamigyō-ku Historical Sites Selection/Shirakumo Shrine - Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== History == Shirakumo Shrine was originally the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} of the {{ill|Saionji family|en|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472|lt=Saionji family|1=Saionji family}}, established in 1224 (Gen'nin 1) by {{ill|Saionji Kintsune|en|Saionji Kintsune|WD=Q3464635|lt=Saionji Kintsune|1=Saionji Kintsune}} as part of the construction of the North Mountain Hall (Hokuzan-den).<ref name="Kyoto City Kamigyō-ku" /> It was later relocated to Akahachiman Kyogoku-ji and then moved to its current location in 1769 (Meiwa 6) along with the Saionji residence. Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi|1=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.
== Branch Shrine == In 1939 (Shōwa 14), Mantarō Usui purchased Saionji Kinmochi's former residence, {{ill|Mansuke-tei|ja|萬介亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mansuke-tei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kinugasa Village, Kyoto. Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Benzaiten Statue (former principal object of worship at Saionji Myōondo) - Designated as an Important Cultural Property on June 26, 2002.<ref name="Important Cultural Property">[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp National Cultural Properties Database] Accessed September 17, 2018.</ref>
== Practical Information == ; location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, inside Kyoto Imperial Palace grounds.
; Access * Nearest station: Approx. 9 minutes on foot (about 900 meters north) from {{ill|Marutamachi Station|en|Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2346816|lt=Marutamachi Station|1=Marutamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Municipal Subway|en|Kyoto Municipal Subway|WD=Q728081|lt=Kyoto City Subway|1=Kyoto Municipal Subway}}.
; Nearby sites * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goō Shrine|1=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|ja|菅原院天満宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine|1=Sugawara-in Tenman-gû|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218751}}

Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Saionji family relocated to {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}, and the shrine became known as "Shirakumo Shrine."
In 1869 (Meiji 2), {{ill|Saionji Kinmochi|en|Saionji Kinmochi|WD=Q297628|lt=Saionji Kinmochi|1=Saionji Kinmochi}} established the {{ill|Private Academy Ritsumeikan|ja|私塾立命館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Academy Ritsumeikan|1=Ritsumeikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594681}} on this site, which later grew into {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.

Usui enshrined a branch spirit of Shirakumo Shrine at this location, naming the site "Shigure-an." The residence and its related landmarks have since been dismantled, with the stone monument marking the "Site of Saionji's Residence" now preserved by {{ill|Ritsumeikan University|en|Ritsumeikan University|WD=Q1152190|lt=Ritsumeikan University|1=Ritsumeikan University}}.<ref>[https://www.ritsumei.ac.jp/archives/column/article.html/?id=45 Nostalgia for Ritsumeikan: Saionji Kinmochi and "Mansuke-tei"] Ritsumeikan Archives, March 17, 2014.</ref>
